markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Drake's "Iceman" album as the frontrunner at near-certainty after its surprise full streaming rollout on Spotify and Apple Music earlier this month, outpacing GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 launch following Rockstar's second delay last fall. GPT-6 holds strong momentum at 66% implied probability amid OpenAI's hints of accelerated AI rollouts, while Rihanna and Playboi Carti new albums trade near 50-55% on their surprise-drop histories. High-volatility bets like Bitcoin hitting $1 million or geopolitical shocks hover around even odds, reflecting tail risks in the six-month window. Rumors swirl of GTA VI Trailer 3 dropping imminently—possibly today—and Best Buy pre-orders launching May 18, alongside Summer Game Fest reveals, but Take-Two affirms the date amid $1 billion+ budget pressures.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects intense uncertainty in Harvey Weinstein's protracted #MeToo-era legal saga, with no additional prison time edging out at 30.4% implied probability amid his third New York rape retrial reaching jury deliberations after a forceful defense summation urging acquittal on May 12. The closely bunched odds—10-20 years (25%) and 20-30 years (24.4%) trailing—stem from his 2025 split verdict conviction on one sex crime count (already appealed), overturned 2020 rape ruling, and pending Los Angeles appeal of a 16-year sentence where judges appeared skeptical in April hearings. Weinstein, detained at Rikers amid health episodes like recent chest pains, faces up to 25 more years if convicted; a not-guilty verdict or successful appeals could enable release or time served, fueling the tight race as traders weigh jury dynamics and next rulings.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes share top billing in the 2026 Stanley Cup futures at 38% and 37% implied probabilities, reflecting trader consensus on their dominant regular-season records—Avalanche topping power rankings—and flawless early playoff executions. Avalanche swept the Los Angeles Kings before dispatching Minnesota Wild in five games to reach the Western Conference Final, buoyed by Nathan MacKinnon's scoring surge and stable goaltending. Hurricanes mirrored the momentum, closing their second-round series via coach Rod Brind'Amour's postgame handshake line, leveraging Seth Jarvis' breakout and defensive depth. Vegas Golden Knights linger at 13.5% amid Jack Eichel's clutch play, while Montreal Canadiens (9.5%) hold a 3-2 edge over Buffalo Sabres (2.1%) in an upset-prone Atlantic clash, keeping the path wide open as conference finals loom May 22.

markets.question: Will the Colorado Avalanche win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Colorado Avalanche win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's flawless 8-0 playoff record, including a 4-0 sweep of the Lakers in the Western Conference semifinals, has solidified trader consensus at 59.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA Championship, bolstered by their league-best 64-18 regular-season mark and rest advantage awaiting the West Finals winner. San Antonio Spurs hold 22.3% after Victor Wembanyama's return sparked a 126-97 Game 5 rout of Minnesota, pushing their series lead to 3-2 ahead of Game 6 today. New York Knicks' 14.1% reflects their dominant 4-0 sweep of Philadelphia, positioning them for the Eastern Conference Finals, while Cleveland Cavaliers' 3-2 edge over Detroit in semis supports 3.4% amid recent overtime heroics. Trailing probabilities for Pistons and Timberwolves stem from their precarious series deficits.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders price France (17%) and Spain (16%) as virtual co-favorites for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, reflecting their dominant UEFA qualifier runs—Spain flawless, France unbeaten—and robust squads entering the expanded 48-team tournament largely injury-free among stars like Mbappé and Yamal. Argentina (9%), as defending champions, holds firm on Messi-led momentum from recent Copa América success, while England's depth (11%) and Brazil's talent (9%) keep the race tight despite Rodrygo's ACL sidelining and Militão's hamstring issue. Power rankings place Spain and France atop with minimal separation, underscoring parity among European and South American powerhouses amid final club-season fitness updates and balanced group-stage paths.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chinese President Xi Jinping's recent high-profile summit with US President Donald Trump on May 14, 2026, including hosting a state dinner and bilateral talks at the Great Hall of the People, underscores his unchallenged leadership and robust public presence, reinforcing trader consensus at 93% against his departure before 2027. Absent any credible reports of health issues, internal party challenges, or succession signals from the Chinese Communist Party elite, Xi continues consolidating power through ongoing diplomatic engagements—like meeting Brunei's crown prince—and legislative actions such as the NPC's March 2026 ethnic unity law. With term limits removed in 2018 and his third term secured at the 2022 Party Congress, traders see minimal barriers to his tenure extending past the 21st Congress in late 2027, barring unforeseen shocks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Gov. Gavin Newsom as the clear frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.1%, driven by his dominant performances in recent California primary polls—like a March UC Berkeley/POLITICO survey where he led Kamala Harris 28%-14% among Democratic-leaning voters—and his national profile as a vocal Trump critic via book tours in early primary states. Harris trails at 9% amid lingering 2024 electability doubts, while Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's 8.8% reflects buzz from a May AtlasIntel poll topping her at 26% nationally, though traders view her progressive appeal as polarizing. The fragmented field, with Sen. Jon Ossoff third at 6.3%, underscores uncertainty ahead of November 2026 midterms, where strong showings by governors like Newsom, Pennsylvania's Josh Shapiro (4.7%), or Transportation Sec. Pete Buttigieg (4.3%) could consolidate endorsements, fundraising, and delegate paths.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Gov. Gavin Newsom as the clear frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.1%, driven by his dominant performances in recent California primary polls—like a March UC Berkeley/POLITICO survey where he led Kamala Harris 28%-14% among Democratic-leaning voters—and his national profile as a vocal Trump critic via book tours in early primary states. Harris trails at 9% amid lingering 2024 electability doubts, while Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's 8.8% reflects buzz from a May AtlasIntel poll topping her at 26% nationally, though traders view her progressive appeal as polarizing. The fragmented field, with Sen. Jon Ossoff third at 6.3%, underscores uncertainty ahead of November 2026 midterms, where strong showings by governors like Newsom, Pennsylvania's Josh Shapiro (4.7%), or Transportation Sec. Pete Buttigieg (4.3%) could consolidate endorsements, fundraising, and delegate paths.
